prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency that sells digital solutions to local service businesses.

Analyze this locksmith's homepage for digital maturity and gaps. You are NOT judging quality — you are identifying sales opportunities.

Critical for locksmiths: a locked-out person on their phone needs to reach help in under 30 seconds.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No click-to-call button visible above the fold
- No service area mentioned
- No emergency availability stated (24/7)
- Slow load (critical for panicked mobile users)
- No trust signals (license, insurance, reviews)
- Generic template with stock photos
- No schema markup

COLD LEAD signals:
- Prominent emergency phone number, click-to-call
- Service area clearly listed
- 24/7 availability stated
- Fast mobile load
- Trust badges: license, insurance, trade body membership
- Real photos of vans, team, work
- LocalBusiness schema with service area

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of digital presence>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = more gaps = better lead>",
  "digitalMaturityScore": "<1-10, higher = more mature>",
  "hasClickToCall": true/false,
  "hasServiceArea": true/false,
  "hasEmergencyInfo": true/false,
  "hasTrustSignals": true/false,
  "hasMobileExperience": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<single biggest gap a web agency could sell>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response: { "records": [ { "url": "https://www.londonlocksmith.co.uk", "pageName": "London Locksmith Homepage", "summary": "An established London locksmith site with phone numbers and WhatsApp links present, but the site appears to be an older, image-heavy design with no visible 24/7 emergency declaration, no explicit service area coverage list, and no trust signals such as licensing or insurance badges. The enquiry form uses a same-day callback model which is poorly suited to emergency lockout scenarios.", "opportunityScore": 7, "digitalMaturityScore": 3, "hasClickToCall": true, "hasServiceArea": false, "hasEmergencyInfo": false, "hasTrustSignals": false, "hasMobileExperience": false, "topOpportunity": "Add a bold above-the-fold emergency 24/7 call-to-action with clear service area coverage and trust badges, replacing the outdated image-based layout with a fast-loading mobile-first design", "notes": "Decent phone accessibility but the dated image-based design, lack of 24/7 messaging, and absence of trust signals leave significant conversion gaps a web agency could directly address." }, { "url": "https://www.londonlocksmith.co.uk/index.html", "pageName": "London Locksmith Index Page", "summary": "Identical content to the root homepage, featuring phone and WhatsApp links rendered as image assets rather than native buttons, with no emergency availability statement, no defined service area, and no visible trust credentials. The same-day enquiry callback model is a critical mismatch for emergency lockout users needing immediate help.", "opportunityScore": 7, "digitalMaturityScore": 3, "hasClickToCall": true, "hasServiceArea": false, "hasEmergencyInfo": false, "hasTrustSignals": false, "hasMobileExperience": false, "topOpportunity": "Rebuild the homepage with a mobile-first emergency UX: sticky click-to-call button, 24/7 availability statement, service area list, and schema markup to replace the outdated image-dependent layout", "notes": "Same gaps as the root URL — phone numbers are technically linked but buried in image assets, and the complete absence of emergency, trust, and locality signals makes this a strong pitch for a full site redesign." } ] }
